title: prunus avium var. bigarella
intro: Prunus avium var. bigarella, also known as Bigarella, is a variety of the European wild cherry tree, native to Europe and Western Asia, with a smaller, more compact growth habit compared to the standard Prunus avium. The bark and leaves of the Prunus avium var. bigarella plant possess medicinal properties, particularly as a source of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The health benefits of Prunus avium var. bigarella include reducing fever, alleviating headaches and joint pain, and acting as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. Prunus avium var. bigarella can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, often used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
